cushing syndrome: Cushing syndrome results from chronic cortisol excess. Germline and somatic variants in PRKAR1A, PDE11A, PDE8B and GNAS disrupt cAMP/PKA signaling, leading to adrenal hyperplasia or cortisol-secreting tumours. PPNAD and micronodular adrenal hyperplasia are archetypal molecular causes.
parkinson's disease: Parkinson's disease is a progressive movement disorder driven by loss of dopaminergic neurons in the substantia nigra. SNCA (alpha-synuclein), LRRK2, PARK7, PINK1 and PRKN variants impair mitochondrial quality control, leading to Lewy body pathology and chronic oxidative stress.
alzheimer's disease: Alzheimer's disease is characterised by amyloid-beta plaques and tau neurofibrillary tangles. APP, PSEN1 and PSEN2 mutations drive familial AD via altered gamma-secretase processing of APP, while APOE4 is the strongest common risk allele.
amyotrophic lateral sclerosis: ALS is a motor neuron disease caused by variants in SOD1, TARDBP (TDP-43), FUS, and C9ORF72 hexanucleotide repeat expansion. Mechanisms include protein aggregation, RNA processing defects, and impaired axonal transport.
acute myeloid leukemia: AML arises from clonal expansion of myeloid progenitors. Driver mutations include FLT3-ITD, NPM1, DNMT3A, IDH1/2 and TET2. These alter epigenetic regulation and STAT/RAS signalling, blocking differentiation.
basal cell carcinoma: BCC is the most common skin cancer, driven almost universally by hedgehog pathway hyperactivation from loss-of-function variants in PTCH1 or activating variants in SMO.
clear cell renal cell carcinoma: ccRCC is characterised by biallelic VHL inactivation, leading to constitutive HIF1alpha/HIF2alpha stabilisation and a hypervascular, pseudo-hypoxic phenotype.
colorectal cancer: CRC progression follows the APC/KRAS/TP53 adenoma-to-carcinoma sequence. Microsatellite-unstable tumours carry MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2 defects; BRAF V600E defines a serrated-pathway subtype.
creutzfeldt-jakob disease: CJD is a fatal prion disease caused by misfolded PrP(Sc). Germline PRNP variants (e.g. E200K, V210I) accelerate conformational conversion from PrP(C).
gaucher disease: Gaucher disease is a lysosomal storage disorder caused by biallelic GBA1 variants that reduce glucocerebrosidase activity, leading to glucosylceramide accumulation in macrophages.
glioblastoma multiforme: GBM carries frequent EGFR amplification, PTEN loss, TERT promoter mutations, and CDKN2A/B deletion. IDH1 status stratifies primary vs secondary GBM.
hepatocellular carcinoma: HCC driver events include CTNNB1 gain-of-function, TP53 loss, AXIN1 inactivation, and TERT promoter mutations. Chronic HBV/HCV infection and NAFLD are major predisposing contexts.
lesch-nyhan syndrome: Lesch-Nyhan syndrome is an X-linked disorder caused by HPRT1 loss-of-function, producing hyperuricemia and characteristic self-injurious behaviour due to disrupted purine salvage.
melanoma: Cutaneous melanoma is dominated by BRAF V600E, NRAS Q61, and NF1 loss-of-function variants converging on MAPK pathway hyperactivation. CDKN2A germline loss confers familial risk.
methylmalonic aciduria and homocystinuri: MMA-HCY cblC type is caused by MMACHC variants, disrupting both methylmalonyl-CoA mutase and methionine synthase cofactor handling.
n-acetylglutamate synthase deficiency: NAGS deficiency is a urea-cycle disorder caused by biallelic NAGS variants, producing hyperammonemia via loss of the essential CPS1 allosteric activator N-acetylglutamate.
non-small cell lung cancer: NSCLC driver events include EGFR exon 19 deletions and L858R, KRAS G12C, ALK/ROS1/RET fusions and MET exon 14 skipping. Targeted inhibitors match each genotype.
pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma: PDAC is driven by near-universal KRAS G12D/V mutations with sequential loss of CDKN2A, TP53 and SMAD4. Germline BRCA2 or PALB2 variants identify targetable subsets.
papillary renal cell carcinoma: Type 1 pRCC features MET germline and somatic variants; type 2 frequently carries FH loss-of-function variants associated with hereditary leiomyomatosis.
pituitary adenoma: Pituitary adenomas include AIP-related familial isolated pituitary adenoma, MEN1-linked adenomas, and sporadic GNAS-mutant somatotroph tumours.
primary aldosteronism: Primary aldosteronism is driven by somatic KCNJ5, CACNA1D, ATP1A1 and ATP2B3 variants in aldosterone-producing adrenal adenomas, causing autonomous mineralocorticoid excess.
prion disease: Prion diseases arise from PRNP variants that promote conversion of cellular prion protein to the misfolded scrapie isoform, with characteristic spongiform neurodegeneration.
spinocerebellar ataxia: SCAs are a heterogeneous family of autosomal-dominant ataxias driven by CAG-repeat expansions (e.g. ATXN1, ATXN2, ATXN3) or channel-gene variants (CACNA1A).
thyroid cancer: Papillary thyroid cancer harbours BRAF V600E and RET/PTC rearrangements; follicular carcinoma is driven by RAS mutations and PAX8-PPARG fusions.
thyroid dyshormonogenesis: Congenital thyroid dyshormonogenesis arises from biallelic variants in TG, TPO, DUOX2, or SLC5A5, impairing thyroid hormone synthesis.
von hippel-lindau syndrome: Von Hippel-Lindau syndrome is caused by germline VHL loss-of-function, producing hemangioblastomas, clear cell renal cell carcinoma, and pheochromocytoma via HIF pathway dysregulation.
